title: Fix for Ascend NPU when using ChatTTS to sample the voice of a real speaker
body:
This PR is a bugfix for Ascend NPU when using ChatTTS to sample the voice of a real speaker.

Problem: Complex dtype used in the process of computing MelSpectrogram is not supported in `torch_npu` now, and we could get an error when sampling the voice of a real speaker.

Environment: Ascend NPU (Atlas 300T A2), CANN 8.0.RC2, torch-npu 2.1.0.post6, torch 2.1.0 on ubuntu 20.04.

The failure stack shows the error occurs in DVAE.sample_audio -> DVAE.forward -> MelSpectrogramFeatures when creating mel spectrograms on the NPU device. The torchaudio stft/transform path can produce complex dtypes which torch_npu cannot handle. The fix is to run torchaudio transforms on CPU and move back the resulting real mel tensor to the target device before further processing.

step_plan.summary: Compute torchaudio MelSpectrogram on CPU inside MelSpectrogramFeatures.forward, then transfer the resulting real mel tensor to the target device to avoid complex/unsupported ops on Ascend NPU (torch_npu).

steps.operation: Run torchaudio MelSpectrogram on CPU and move result to target device
steps.reason: Torchaudio's STFT/transform path may produce complex dtypes or call kernels not supported by torch_npu when run on Ascend NPU. Computing the MelSpectrogram on CPU avoids producing complex/unsupported tensors on the NPU; after obtaining the real-valued mel tensor, move it to the DVAE/device for subsequent processing.
steps.side_effects: Introduces a CPU<->NPU tensor copy (audio -> CPU prior to mel computation, mel -> device afterwards) which may slightly increase latency. Unit/integration tests that exercise DVAE.sample_audio and MelSpectrogramFeatures should be run and may need to account for the device transfer; behavior and API remain unchanged. Downstream modules that assume the mel computation ran in-place on the device will see identical tensor dtype and shape but may observe timing differences. Ensure no other code relies on torchaudio transforms running on the NPU; this change is safe for CPU-only and GPU environments but moves torchaudio work to CPU even if self.device is GPU/NPU.
